source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Name the lowest electric multipole in the radiation field emitted by the following time-varying charge distributions. A uniform charged spherical shell whose radius varies as $R=R_{0}+R_{1}\cos(\omega t)$.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: all the electric multipole moments are zero.

multi_step: The task requires identifying the symmetry of a uniformly charged spherical shell with time-varying radius and relating that symmetry to electric multipole radiation.
This is not mere lookup: it combines the distribution description with a physics rule about which electric multipole moments can be nonzero or radiative.
The response is short, but deriving it plausibly involves at least two conceptual steps rather than direct extraction.
